mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents using standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead for agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent deployments in production environments.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is essential for managing your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on your preferred cloud provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Kagenti application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> This command installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Drain all pods in the Kagenti namespace.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure that the Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install the release: ... already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a release with the same name already exists in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Use 'helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti' to remove the existing release before reinstalling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster creation command and check the cloud provider's console for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff' on Kagenti pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kagenti image cannot be pulled, possibly due to incorrect image tags or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image repository and ensure that the image tag is correct and accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
